behavior_summary: Provides handlers for a tall candlestick item: when the player looks at the item it announces its short name, when the item is 'used' it toggles or cycles a visual state encoded in the item's frame bits, and an animation/auxiliary method can be spawned to drive ongoing visual behavior.
TALLCAND::look (Event 0) calls Item::bark(this_item_process, "candlestick ") to present the object's descriptive name to the player and then suspends/returns.

TALLCAND::use (Event 1) reads the item's frame (Item::getFrame), inspects the low two bits: if they are 0 it increments the frame (frame+1), otherwise it clears the low two bits (frame & 0xFC); the new frame is written back with Item::setFrame. This implements a toggle/cycle of the visual state (e.g., lit/unlit or state stages).

TALLCAND::anim (Event 2) sets per-process info/exclusion and spawns METHOD::0610 for the item process, indicating an animation or helper method is started to handle timed/animated behavior for the candlestick.
notable_control_flow: Look handler: simple straight-line control — set process info/exclusion, call Item::bark, then suspend/return.
Use handler: read-modify-write pattern on an item-local frame value; decision branch based on (frame & 3) to either increment or clear low bits before calling Item::setFrame.
Anim handler: non-blocking spawn of METHOD::0610 (animation/helper) for the item process, then suspend — indicates asynchronous animation behavior is delegated to a spawned method.
No global flags are read or written by these handlers; state is object-local (frame bits) and behavior is driven by spawned method(s) and frame updates.
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning do the low two bits of the frame encode? (Likely lit/unlit or stage index, but not explicit.)
What precise behavior METHOD::0610 implements when spawned (e.g., flicker animation, timed light decay, interaction with world lighting)?
What specific process info/exclusion fields are set by the handlers and whether they affect other engine systems (collision, AI processing, visibility)?
Are there other events or systems that toggle the same frame bits (interactions, time of day, other items)?

main_function.name: U8P_TALLCAND::look (Event 0)
main_function.purpose: When the player or relevant actor examines the candlestick, present the object's short descriptive name via the item's bark/description routine.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the Look event for the candlestick item (player inspects or examines the object).
VM supplies this_item_process (object process) and pid (viewer process) as parameters.

function_map.name: U8P_TALLCAND::look (Event 0)
function_map.role: Look/inspect handler that announces the item's short name to the viewer via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_TALLCAND::use (Event 1)
function_map.role: Use handler that toggles or cycles the item's visual/frame state by manipulating the low two bits of the frame and writing it back.

function_map.name: U8P_TALLCAND::anim (Event 2)
function_map.role: Animation/auxiliary handler that spawns METHOD::0610 for the item process to run ongoing visual/temporal behavior.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided routine to display or emit a short text message from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Engine-provided accessor returning the item's current frame/state value.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine-provided mutator to set the item's frame/state value.

function_map.name: METHOD::0610
function_map.role: Spawnable method id used for candlestick animation/auxiliary behavior (specifics inferred but not present in this script).

external_calls.purpose: Present the item's short descriptive text ('candlestick ') to the viewer process; primary visible feedback when the player examines the object.
Read the current frame/state of the item to determine which branch of the toggle/cycle to execute.
Write the updated frame after incrementing or clearing low bits to update the item's visual state.
Spawn an asynchronous animation/auxiliary method to handle timed changes or animation for the candlestick process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides handlers for a tall candlestick item: when the player looks at the item it announces its short name, when the item is 'used' it toggles or cycles a visual state encoded in the item's frame bits, and an animation/auxiliary method can be spawned to drive ongoing visual behavior.
